data.body:
G3 (child coverage) container review — parent 5fd2 × child 75a9 (Pass-4 affirmative coach): NO FINDINGS (clean pairing).

4-bucket coverage audit of the parent criteria this child is responsible for / adjacent to:

FULLY COVERED by 75a9:
- Parent AC "Pass-4 affirmative COACH: single-turn structured-output call over surviving findings; project-extensible move registry (moves 1-9,11,12, LOCKED templates); {move_id, subject, finding_refs} output; deterministic template rendering; caller-visible finding id for reference-not-restate; C1-C4 guardrails; advisory only; shared with 9da1" → maps 1:1 onto child AC1-5. In-scope, no over/under-reach.
- Parent Pass-4 design-summary text (subject validator enforcing C1, advisory-never-blocks, consumes orchestrator-minted id) → covered.

COVERED-BY-A-NAMED-CONSUMER (so NOT an absence against this pairing — verified against the whole roster):
- Unified priority score + advisory cap (default 10, BLOCKING exempt) → owned by sibling 487d (Pass-3; confirmed DEFAULT_ADVISORY_CAP=10, blocking exempt). 75a9 consumes the capped surfaced set.
- Finding id minting ("SOLE owner") → owned by sibling ca03 (mint_finding_id, confirmed). 75a9 is an explicit CONSUMER.
- REVIEW_RESULT records the same id → owned by sibling db7b (confirmed). 
- Coaching-spec finding-authoring AC (grounded/specific/ranked/affirming/checklist) → principally a Pass-1 property owned by sibling 1913; Pass-4 is the distinct affirmative-MOVE layer, enumerated as a separate parent AC.

PARTIAL (noted, NOT an absence — no sibling owns it; it is intrinsic to 75a9): parent Pass-4 text + child AC1 say output is "CLUSTERED by move," but the child's own completion-verification + the ca03/T8 reviews record the implemented schema as one-note-per-finding with no clustering step (downgraded to a consistency note). Flagging as a partial-coverage nuance of the parent's "clustered by move" requirement, not an uncovered parent criterion.

UNCOVERED: none attributable to this pairing.

Net: clean for G3 — the child covers exactly its designated parent criteria; every adjacent parent criterion it defers is demonstrably covered by a named sibling (487d/ca03/db7b/1913); no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered that this pairing should flag.
